steps.title: Apply the sample-size threshold
steps.content: The passage says a handedness study needs at least 176 individuals to produce a representative result. Chen's gorilla study and Brooks's chimpanzee study each included only 8 individuals, so both are far below the stated threshold for drawing representative conclusions.

steps.title: Reject one-sided reliability
steps.content: A and D treat Chen's study as reliable despite the tiny sample. B assumes Brooks underestimated right-handedness, which the small sample cannot show. Since both studies fall far below the threshold, neither supports a meaningful comparison. The answer is C.
